analysis.consistency: Consistent with standard D&D 5e feature presentation. The structure clearly defines the feature's name, source class, level requirement, and mechanical effect.
analysis.quality: High quality. The description is clear, concise, and directly quotes the source material (implied by the exact wording). It specifies action cost, duration scaling, range scaling based on favored terrain, and the exact types of creatures sensed.

mechanics.resource_cost: One Ranger Spell Slot (Slot level determines duration)
mechanics.duration_scaling: 1 minute per level of the spell slot expended (e.g., casting with a 3rd level slot grants 3 minutes).
mechanics.range: 1 mile base range, up to 6 miles if in favored terrain.
mechanics.effect: Senses the *presence* (not location or number) of Aberrations, Celestials, Dragons, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead within range.
mechanics.notable_interaction: The range extension via favored terrain is a significant buff tied directly to the Ranger's core class identity.

analysis.suggestions: No mechanical changes suggested; the feature is well-defined as per official ruleset interpretations.
